text.title: Launch RACI Template Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Launch RACI Template methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each workstream row MUST have exactly one Accountable (A); rows with zero or multiple A entries are rejected.
rule.rationale: multiple A's mean nobody is accountable in practice.

rule.statement: Responsible (R) and Accountable (A) MAY be the same person; rows where R is empty are rejected.
rule.rationale: R-empty means 'nobody is doing the work', which is the failure mode RACI exists to prevent.

rule.statement: Every named assignment MUST cite the source (engagement charter, role-card, prior-launch retro); un-sourced names are flagged 'verify-before-launch'.
rule.rationale: names dragged in by the agent without source are the #1 cause of 'I never agreed to that' on launch day.

rule.statement: Workstream list MUST cover pre-launch (T-14 to T-1), launch-day (T0), and post-launch (T+1 to T+14) phases; RACIs that stop at T0 are rejected.
rule.rationale: most launch failures happen post-T0 and have no owner because RACI stopped at the launch event.

rule.statement: Consulted (C) implies two-way dialogue before action; Informed (I) implies one-way after action; mixing them is forbidden.
rule.rationale: C/I conflation degrades the artefact into a contact list and the lead loses signal.

rule.statement: At least one launch rehearsal MUST validate the RACI end-to-end before T0; rehearsal findings update the RACI in writing.
rule.rationale: RACIs that haven't been rehearsed have a high defect rate at first contact with launch reality.
